Q5 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

An opaque solid cone is suspendended in the path of a parallel beam of light such that its shadow is cast on a screen oriented perpendicular to the direction of the light beam.
Under these conditions, which one of the shadows A, B, C and D is not possible?

 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• A cone’s circular base can project a circle when the cone’s axis is perpendicular to the screen.
• Tilt the cone a bit and the circle becomes an ellipse.
• If the cone is oriented so the apex and base edge project on the screen, the outline can form a
triangle C the lateral surface projects as two straight sloping edges meeting at the apex.

Solution

Q8 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The diameter of a sphere is decreased by 25%. By what percentage does its curved surface area decrease?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
If the diameter decreases by 25%, the radius also decreases by 25%.
So the new radius = 75% of the old radius = 0.75r
Curved surface area is proportional to the square of the radius : (0.75)2 = 0.5625
This means the new area is 56.25% of the original area
So, the decrease = 100 – 56.25 = 43.75%
Therefore, the curved surface area decreases by 43.75%.

Q27 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Randomized exponential back-off is a mechanism used to stabilize contention MAC protocols.
Which of the following statements is correct?

Correct answer: B, A, C, D
Solution

(b)
(a) False, they might get unlucky and back-off the same amount.
(b) True, the probability of a repeat collision is a smaller in each subsequent backoff.
(c) False, it can be used in any contention period.
(d) False, it can be used in any contention period.

Q30 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following problems are undecidable?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
(a) Equivalent to the Halting Problem. It is undecidable because we cannot predict in general
whether a program will halt and produce output.
(b) CFLs are not closed under complement, and there is no algorithm to decide if the complement
of a given CFL is also CFL hence, this problem is undecidable.
(c) Regular languages are closed under complement, and we can construct the complement DFA
from a given DFA. This problem is decidable.
(d) Recursive languages are closed under complement, so membership in I can always be decided.
This problem is decidable.

Q48 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Assuming that malloc is successful in each of these for allocating memory, select all options which are likely to create problems with memory i.e., runtime error or memory leak.

 
Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
(a) Writing into freed memory is illegal. Dangling pointer.
(b) Here in the last line, the location of pointer is changed to 0 after it is set to NULL. This is valid
since the value at the memory location pointed by NULL pointer is not accessed.
(c) Memory leak as the first int is not freed.
(d) This is illegal as memory assigned for char but int is assigned.

Q65 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statement(s). Then, which of the following is/are true?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• Maskable Interrupt: An Interrupt that can be disabled or ignored by the instructions of CPU are called as Maskable Interrupt.
• Non-Maskable Interrupt: An interrupt that cannot be disabled or ignored by the instructions
of CPU are called as Non-Maskable Interrupt.
• Vectored Interrupt: Device causing interrupt provides the starting address of Interrupt Service Routine (ISR).
• Non-vectored Interrupt: Device causing interrupt does not provides the starting address of Interrupt Service Routine (ISR).
